description: Authorized Copilot Chat model labels for APEX agents. Two-part design: `models` is hand-maintained metadata that authorizes which labels may appear in agent frontmatter; `assignments` is auto-generated from frontmatter (the canonical source) so the catalog can never drift from the agents themselves. The agent registry mirrors frontmatter via validate-model-consistency.mjs; the catalog enforces the label allow-list and assignment integrity via validate-model-catalog.mjs.

Claude Opus 4.7 (High reasoning).notes: Highest-reasoning tier. Reserve for steps with creative or multi-artifact decisions.

Claude Opus 4.6.notes: Deprecated 2026-05 in favor of Claude Opus 4.7 (High reasoning). Retained for audit history; not authorized for new agents.

Claude Sonnet 4.6.notes: Active for Step 3 Design and the IaC validation/preview subagents (bicep-validate, bicep-whatif, terraform-validate, terraform-plan) after the 2026-05 GPT-5.4 → Sonnet 4.6 subagent migration. The Anthropic prompting style (XML-tagged blocks, role-first, quote-grounded review, checklist-driven structured output) suits both ADR + diagram authoring and the structured PASS/FAIL findings produced by the validation subagents. Other Sonnet-cohort agents (Orchestrator Fast Path, Governance, CodeGen, Challenger) moved to GPT-5.5 in the same cohort migration.

GPT-5.4.notes: Strong structured output + tool use. Use for validators and deterministic subagents.

GPT-5.5.notes: Outcome-first prompting per the OpenAI GPT-5.5 prompting guide: explicit Role / Personality / Goal / Success / Constraints / Output / Stop sections, retrieval budgets, decision rules over absolutes, and stopping conditions. More efficient reasoning than predecessors — re-evaluate low/medium effort before escalating to high.

GPT-5.3-Codex.notes: Code/maths-focused. Used by cost-estimate-subagent for numerical reasoning.

governance.source_of_truth: Agent frontmatter is canonical. The agent registry mirrors frontmatter (enforced by validate-model-consistency.mjs). The catalog `models` block authorizes which labels are allowed; the catalog `assignments` block is generated from frontmatter and must match it exactly (enforced by validate-model-catalog.mjs).
rules: Every model label appearing in agent or subagent frontmatter MUST exist as a key in `models`.
Every model label appearing in the agent registry MUST exist as a key in `models`.
The `assignments` block MUST match the output of `node tools/scripts/generate-model-catalog.mjs`. A lefthook pre-commit hook regenerates it whenever an agent frontmatter file is staged.
A model with `deprecated: true` MAY remain in `models` for audit history but MUST NOT appear in any active assignment (frontmatter or registry).

assignments.description: Auto-generated inventory of agent → model assignments derived from frontmatter (canonical source). Do not edit by hand; run `node tools/scripts/generate-model-catalog.mjs` or let the lefthook pre-commit hook refresh it when frontmatter changes.
